Many callers make it worse by wrapping AppInput in their own bare `<label class="form-label">` (DietModal.vue lines 13-17), which still associates with nothing.

top_findings.title: Tab-based faux routing gives screen reader users no navigation feedback at all
top_findings.evidence: src/App.vue line 18 (`<component :is="routeMap[ui.currentTab]" />` swaps entire pages with no focus management, no document.title change, no announcement); src/components/layout/BottomNav.vue (nav buttons carry no aria-current/aria-selected; active state is color-only via .nav-tab.active in style.css line 337); AnimalDetail's 11 sub-tabs and the ARCHITECTURE.md tab pattern use plain buttons with an .active class — grep shows zero role="tab"/tablist/aria-selected anywhere in src/
top_findings.detail: The app deliberately avoids a router (ARCHITECTURE.md 'Tab-Based Navigation'), so activating a nav item silently replaces the whole view: focus stays on the clicked button, the page title never changes, and nothing is announced. A screen reader user pressing 'Animals' gets no confirmation the view changed (WCAG 4.1.2, 2.4.2). The color-only active state also fails 1.4.1 for low-vision users.

top_findings.title: Icon-only controls lack accessible names; decorative emoji are announced as content
top_findings.evidence: src/components/layout/MoreDrawer.vue line 14 (close button content is bare '✕', no aria-label); src/components/layout/Header.vue lines 16-38 (icon buttons rely on title attribute only); BottomNav.vue lines 31-52 and MoreDrawer sections use emoji icons (🏠 🐾 🩺 💊) without aria-hidden — grep shows aria-hidden appears exactly once in the entire components tree (TasksPage.vue line 56); the inbox unread nav-badge is a bare visual span
top_findings.detail: Screen readers announce '✕' as 'multiplication X' and read every emoji's Unicode name before each nav label ('dog paw prints, Animals'), creating constant noise in the app's primary navigation. Header controls named only by title are unreliable on touch and for many AT combinations.

top_findings.title: Touch targets and text sizes below field-use thresholds in core mobile surfaces
top_findings.evidence: src/components/layout/MoreDrawer.vue: 4-column grid (line 168) with feature-btn padding 10px 6px and 9px uppercase labels (line 193), section labels 10px (line 158); src/style.css line 767 checkboxes/radios fixed at 16px; drawer close button padding 4px 6px (~24px target); Toast.vue white-space:nowrap risks clipping long error messages; base font token is 13px with --font-size-xs at 11px used for nav labels
top_findings.detail: This app is explicitly used by volunteers doing rounds, feeding, and med runs on phones — often outdoors, one-handed, possibly gloved. 16px checkboxes, ~24px close buttons, and 9-10px uppercase text fall well short of the 44x44px / legible-text bar (WCAG 2.5.8 and mobile best practice). Credit where due: AppInput and AppSelect do enforce min-height 44px.

top_findings.title: Light-theme mint token likely fails contrast where used as text/active indicator
top_findings.evidence: src/design-tokens.css line 260: light-mode --color-mint is #0FA87D on white/near-white surfaces (#FFFFFF, #F4F1FF); used as text color for active nav tabs (style.css line 338) and MoreDrawer section labels (mint at 10px)
top_findings.detail: #0FA87D on #FFFFFF is roughly 2.9:1 — below the 4.5:1 requirement for the small (10-12px) text it colors. The dark theme is generally strong (white-on-#1A1033 text scale computes comfortably above AA), and the tokens file claims 'WCAG AA+ contrast ratios', but the light-mode mint and the 0.64-alpha tertiary text at 11px sizes deserve a measured audit pass.

revamp_recommendations.title: Fix the four primitives and inherit accessibility everywhere
revamp_recommendations.detail: Because the app funnels through a tiny set of primitives, four targeted retrofits fix hundreds of screens at once: (1) AppButton — replace outline:none with a :focus-visible rule using the existing --shadow-focus token, add aria-busy when loading; (2) AppInput/AppSelect — generate a unique id, wire label[for], add aria-invalid and aria-describedby to error/hint, convert the clearable div to a real <button aria-label="Clear">; (3) Toast.vue — add role="status" aria-live="polite" (assertive/role=alert for error type) and drop white-space:nowrap; (4) ConfirmDialog — role="dialog" aria-modal, focus the confirm button on open, trap Tab, handle Escape, return focus on close. This is days of work, not months, and should precede any powerhouse feature expansion.

revamp_recommendations.title: Build one AppModal/AppDrawer component and migrate the ~20 hand-rolled overlays
revamp_recommendations.detail: Create a shared AppModal (and AppDrawer for MoreDrawer/PersonDetailDrawer) with dialog semantics, focus trap, Escape, scroll lock, and labelled-by wiring, then migrate DietModal, AnimalDetail's inline edit/post/card modals, GrantDetailModal, DeleteMessageDialog, PeopleFilterPanel, QuarantinePage overlays, etc. Add this to NEW_FEATURE_CHECKLIST.md so every new powerhouse module (fundraising, compliance, comms) gets accessible dialogs for free instead of copy-pasting the current broken pattern.

revamp_recommendations.title: Adopt a real router (or add navigation announcements) before the app grows further
revamp_recommendations.detail: The ui.currentTab system already strains at ~40 stores and 30+ admin pages, and it is the root cause of the silent-navigation failure. Moving to vue-router gives URLs, back-button support, deep links from website-originated emails ('view application #123'), and a natural hook for focus management and document.title updates. If a router is too disruptive, minimally: on tab change set document.title, move focus to the new page's h1 (tabindex="-1"), announce via a live region, and add aria-current="page" to BottomNav plus proper role=tablist/tab/tabpanel + arrow-key support to AnimalDetail's 11 sub-tabs.

revamp_recommendations.title: Field-use pass: touch targets, text size, and glove/sunlight ergonomics
revamp_recommendations.detail: For the volunteers doing rounds/feeding/med-runs on phones: enforce 44px minimum targets (MoreDrawer grid to 3 columns with taller cells, checkboxes/radios to 24px+ with padded labels, drawer close to 44px), raise the 9-11px uppercase micro-text to 12px+ minimum, verify light-theme mint (#0FA87D) against its actual surfaces and darken to ~#0A8A65 where used as text, and audit the amber/coral toast backgrounds. This directly serves the powerhouse goal — data quality in feeding/med logs depends on staff being able to hit the right control outdoors.

revamp_recommendations.title: Unify the application pipeline and make it accessible end-to-end
revamp_recommendations.detail: Pipe website adoption/foster/volunteer submissions (currently landing only in the website's Postgres via _worker.js) into the app's applications store the same way animals already sync via animalsAdmin.js — either a webhook writing to Firestore or extending the existing /api/admin proxy pattern. Then build a single Applications module in SanctuaryBase with an accessible review queue (proper table semantics, status announced via live region) and applicant status-notification emails. Right now an applicant with a disability who completes the public form gets no confirmation surface in either system, and staff re-key data between two UIs.

revamp_recommendations.title: Add an app-wide live-region + loading-state service to the ui store
revamp_recommendations.detail: Extend src/stores/ui.js with an announce(message, politeness) action backed by a persistent visually-hidden live region in App.vue, and use it for: navigation changes, list filter results ('12 animals shown'), async load completion (SkeletonLoader currently swaps content silently — add aria-busy on containers), and Firestore real-time updates that mutate visible lists. As dashboards and finance stats grow, this is the difference between a powerhouse that works with a screen reader and one that is a silent wall of updating widgets.

revamp_recommendations.title: Wire accessibility into CI and the feature checklist
revamp_recommendations.detail: Add eslint-plugin-vuejs-accessibility to the lint step (it would have caught the unassociated labels and click-only divs), an axe-core smoke test against the key pages in Vitest, and an 'Accessibility' section in NEW_FEATURE_CHECKLIST.md (labels associated, focus visible, dialog trapped, status announced, 44px targets). Also update DESIGN_SYSTEM.md's 'WCAG AA+' claim to reflect measured ratios per token pair per theme.

strengths: AppInput and AppSelect enforce min-height: 44px, meeting touch-target guidance for the app's most-used form controls (src/components/ui/AppInput.vue line 105, AppSelect.vue line 80)
prefers-reduced-motion media queries already exist in style.css (line 1022) and in AlertBox, AppCard, EmptyState, and StatCard — motion sensitivity was considered

AlertBox.vue ships with role="alert", and the auth flow (VolunteerSignup.vue, WelcomeScreen.vue) uses role="alert" on error banners plus a dynamic aria-label on the password-visibility toggle — the newest components show growing awareness

A genuine token system (design-tokens.css) with semantic text tiers, a defined --shadow-focus token, and full light/dark theme support means contrast and focus fixes are cheap: change tokens once, inherit everywhere
BottomNav uses a semantic <nav> element with real <button>s (not divs), and visible text labels accompany every icon in primary navigation

The animals app-to-website sync (animalsAdmin.js + WebsiteListingTab, with siteAnimalId linking and importFromSite reconciliation) is a well-designed integration pattern worth extending to applications and donations rather than replacing

expert: Donorbox-style fundraising specialist (high-converting nonprofit donation experiences and donor management)
integration_assessment: The website and app connect for content and inbox, but NOT for money or donors. The website (donate.html, fundraiser.html, corporate.html) is a well-crafted persuasion layer whose every donation CTA is an outbound link to a single Zeffy hosted form ("animals-deserve-second-chances", with UTM params). No donation ever touches the site's own backend: _worker.js (Hono + Neon/Drizzle, functions/api/[[path]].ts bundled at ~line 14537) has tables only for animals, contacts, auction_items, suggestions, and newsletter_subscribers — there is no donations, donors, or pledges table and no Zeffy/Stripe webhook endpoint. SanctuaryBase reaches back into the website via a server-side proxy (SB-v2 functions/api/site/[[path]].js forwarding to https://sfr-rescue.pages.dev/api with SITE_ADMIN_TOKEN, role-gated by _shared/verifyRole.js) — a genuinely good pattern used for animals, auction items, contact inbox, and newsletter list. But donor data flows nowhere: Zeffy holds the real transaction/recurring-gift history in its own silo; the app's donors Firestore collection is populated only by hand via DonorsPage.vue's "Log Donation" form; the ledger/financeStats collections are separate again and never reconciled with actual gifts. The result is three disconnected sources of financial truth (Zeffy, Firestore donors, Firestore ledger/financeStats) plus contact-form leads (subject enum includes 'donate', 'in-kind donation', 'car donation') that never convert into donor records. The impact-slider on donate.html even captures amount + monthly intent and then throws it away — window.open to the generic Zeffy form without passing the amount.

top_findings.title: No donation data pipeline: gifts live only inside Zeffy
top_findings.evidence: donate.html lines 156, 444, 462, 487, 575, 670 — every CTA is https://www.zeffy.com/en-US/donation-form/animals-deserve-second-chances; _worker.js (functions/api/[[path]].ts section, lines ~14538-14607) defines no donations/donors table and no webhook route; grep for 'zeffy' in SB-v2 src/ and functions/ returns nothing.
top_findings.detail: There is zero programmatic link between the payment processor and either codebase. No Zeffy webhook, no scheduled import, no CSV ingestion. The org cannot see donor lifetime value, lapsed donors, recurring-gift health, or reconcile revenue — everything requires logging into Zeffy's dashboard. This is the single biggest blocker to a fundraising powerhouse.

top_findings.title: Donor 'CRM' is a flat, denormalized card list with dead buttons
top_findings.evidence: SB-v2 src/components/features/admin/DonorsPage.vue lines 112-128 (addDonor writes totalDonations as a string like '$50', donationCount: 1, lastDonation: 'Today', isMajor: false) and lines 65-66 ('Send Thank You' and 'View History' AppButtons have no @click handlers); StatCard 'This Year' hardcoded to "0" (line 7); src/stores/donors.js is generic CRUD with no donation subcollection, no dedupe, no aggregation.
top_findings.detail: Logging a second gift from the same person creates a duplicate donor document because addDonor always inserts. Amounts are stored as display strings, so no math (totals, segments, YTD) is possible. The thank-you and history features that make a CRM a CRM are non-functional stubs. There is no donations collection at all — a donor and a donation are conflated into one record.

top_findings.title: Donation amount and monthly intent captured on the website are discarded at handoff
top_findings.evidence: donate.html lines 391-448: the impact slider tracks amount and monthlyToggle state, fires SF.track('Donate Click', {amount, monthly}), then window.open()s the generic Zeffy URL with no amount/frequency parameters.
top_findings.detail: The user picks $150/monthly, clicks 'Donate $150 now', and lands on a blank Zeffy form where they must re-choose everything. Every step re-entered costs conversion (industry data: pre-filled amounts lift completion 15-30%). Zeffy supports amount/frequency URL params or per-campaign forms — the highest-ROI one-line fix in the entire stack. Also, SF.track (app.js line 101) is a no-op because no analytics block is uncommented, so even donate-intent data isn't being collected.

top_findings.title: Grant tracking is in-memory only — all pipeline data lost on page refresh
top_findings.evidence: SB-v2 src/components/features/admin/GrantsPage.vue lines 167-170: grants, uploadedFiles, pendingApplications, fundedGrants are plain ref([]) with no Firestore store, no localStorage, no onMounted fetch; GrantFinderPage.vue reads a 1,469-line hardcoded static array (grantData.js) of grants with 2026 deadlines that will silently go stale.
top_findings.detail: A volunteer who enters five grant applications and closes the tab loses everything. The AI LOI generator (functions/api/generate-loi.js, Workers AI) is genuinely useful, but its output isn't saved anywhere either. Grant tracking needs a real grants store + Firestore collection with statuses (prospect → LOI → applied → awarded/declined), deadlines, reporting obligations, and award-to-ledger linkage.

top_findings.title: Three unreconciled sources of financial truth
top_findings.evidence: SB-v2 src/stores/ledger.js (collection 'ledgers'), src/stores/financeStats.js (collection 'financeStats', duplicating income/expense categorization logic), src/stores/donors.js (collection 'donors' with string amounts) — none reference each other; ledger.schema.json categories include 'Donations' and 'Grants & Funding' but nothing links a ledger row to a donor or grant record.
top_findings.detail: financeStats and ledger are near-identical parallel systems (both income/expense docs with category/amount) — a data-model smell that guarantees drift. A logged donation on DonorsPage does not create a ledger entry; a Zeffy payout appears in neither. Accurate 990 prep, donor-restricted-fund tracking, and campaign ROI are impossible on this foundation.

top_findings.title: No receipts, acknowledgments, or year-end statements anywhere
top_findings.evidence: donate.html FAQ line 541 tells donors to 'Request a donation receipt by emailing contact@saintfrancisrescue.org with proof of your donation' while the hero (line 161) promises 'Tax-deductible receipt sent automatically' — contradictory; corporate.html line 154 promises 'A written acknowledgment letter... issued promptly' with no system behind it; the only email machinery in SB-v2 is functions/api/send-reminder.js (MailChannels, task reminders only).
top_findings.detail: Zeffy sends its own transaction receipt, but check/ACH/in-kind/car donations (all promoted on donate.html's channel cards) have no receipt path except manual email. There is no IRS-compliant year-end giving statement generation, no thank-you sequencing, and the on-page promises don't match reality — a donor-trust risk for an org whose whole pitch is transparency.

top_findings.title: Auction has no bidding mechanism — bids go through the contact form or Facebook
top_findings.evidence: fundraiser.html lines 151-201: auctionCard() renders 'Inquire to bid' linking to contact.html?subject=donate&about=Bid:..., or a raw bidInstructions URL; auction_items schema in _worker.js (line 14581) has startingBidCents but no bids table, no currentBid, no bidder identity; SB-v2 auctionItems store is item CRUD only.
top_findings.detail: Current high bid isn't displayed, so bidders can't compete — the core dynamic of an auction. Bids arrive as free-text contact messages a volunteer must manually collate. No winner notification, no payment capture, no receipt (auction purchases have quid-pro-quo tax rules requiring FMV disclosure, which estimatedValueCents could power but doesn't). Fine for a 5-item Facebook-era auction; a ceiling on fundraising events at any scale.

top_findings.title: Donation-intent leads (major gifts, corporate, in-kind, vehicle) die in a shared inbox
top_findings.evidence: _worker.js contactsTable (line 14570) with subject enum 'donate'/'in-kind donation'/'car donation'; SB-v2 src/services/contactsService.js fetches these into a generic Inbox with reply/archive only; no path from a contact row to a donors record, task, or pipeline stage.
top_findings.detail: corporate.html and fundraiser.html deliberately drive high-value conversations ('Talk to us about a major gift', kennel capital campaign section) into contact.html. Those leads land beside name suggestions and general questions with no follow-up workflow, owner, or stage. Major-gift and corporate pipelines are exactly where a small sanctuary's transformative money comes from.

revamp_recommendations.title: Build a unified Gift + Donor data model in Firestore (the CRM core)
revamp_recommendations.detail: Replace the flat donors collection with: donors (identity: name, emails[], phones, address, type: individual/business/foundation, tags, notes, mergeable) and a separate donations collection (donorId, amountCents as integer, date, method: zeffy/check/ach/in-kind/vehicle/auction, frequency: one-time/recurring, campaignId, fund/restriction, softCreditId, acknowledgedAt, receiptSentAt). Derive rollups (lifetimeGiving, ytd, lastGiftDate, giftCount, avgGift) via Cloudflare Function or Firestore trigger rather than hand-maintained strings. Rewrite donors.js/DonorsPage.vue around this: dedupe-by-email on entry, gift history timeline per donor, working 'Send Thank You' and 'View History'. Every other module below depends on this.

revamp_recommendations.title: Ingest Zeffy transactions automatically
revamp_recommendations.detail: Zeffy exposes payout/donation exports and (via their API/Zapier) transaction feeds. Add an SB-v2 Cloudflare Function (functions/api/zeffy-sync.js or a webhook receiver) that upserts each Zeffy transaction into donations, matching donors by email and creating them if new; flag recurring subscriptions with status (active/failed/cancelled) so lapsed recurring donors surface for recovery outreach. Fall back to a CSV import screen if API access is limited. This single integration turns the empty CRM into a live one with zero volunteer data entry and finally reconciles online giving with the ledger.

revamp_recommendations.title: Pass amount and frequency through to checkout, and instrument the funnel
revamp_recommendations.detail: On donate.html, append the slider's amount and monthly flag to the Zeffy URL (or create dedicated one-time/monthly Zeffy forms per amount tier) so the checkout is pre-filled — one line in the donateBtn handler (line 444). Uncomment and configure the Plausible block already scaffolded in every page head so SF.track's Donate Click events (with amount/monthly/location, already wired in app.js lines 101-113) actually record; add a thank-you/return page as a conversion endpoint. Longer term, evaluate embedding the Zeffy form inline on donate.html instead of a new-tab handoff — every context switch loses donors.

revamp_recommendations.title: Automated receipts, acknowledgments, and year-end statements
revamp_recommendations.detail: Extend the existing MailChannels email plumbing (functions/api/send-reminder.js pattern) into a receipts service: on any non-Zeffy donation logged in the app (check, ACH, in-kind with estimated value, vehicle, auction win), auto-send an IRS-compliant acknowledgment (EIN 99-0599742, no-goods-or-services language, or FMV disclosure for auction quid-pro-quo) and stamp receiptSentAt. Add a January job that generates consolidated year-end giving statements per donor across all channels. Fix the donate.html FAQ/hero contradiction about receipts to describe the real behavior. This delivers the transparency the corporate.html copy already promises.

revamp_recommendations.title: Campaigns, funds, and pledges module
revamp_recommendations.detail: New Pinia store + Firestore collections: campaigns (name, goal, dates, e.g. 'Kennel Facility Capital Campaign' already marketed on fundraiser.html lines 137-148) and pledges (donorId, totalAmount, schedule, fulfilled installments). Tag every donation with campaignId/fund so restricted gifts are trackable. Expose campaign progress via the existing site proxy so the website can render a live thermometer on donate.html/fundraiser.html — public goal meters are among the highest-converting patterns in nonprofit fundraising and the plumbing (SF API client + admin proxy) already exists.

revamp_recommendations.title: Persist and pipeline grants; keep the AI LOI generator
revamp_recommendations.detail: Create a grants Firestore collection + store (mirroring the CRUD pattern of donors.js) so GrantsPage.vue's in-memory refs (lines 167-170) survive refresh. Model stages (prospect → LOI sent → application → awarded/declined → reporting due) with deadline reminders through the existing send-reminder function. Save generate-loi.js output onto the grant record. Replace the static 2026-dated grantData.js catalog with an editable prospect list. On award, auto-create a ledger income entry ('Grants & Funding') linking grantId — closing the loop with finance.

revamp_recommendations.title: Merge ledger and financeStats into one finance module tied to donors/grants
revamp_recommendations.detail: ledger.js ('ledgers') and financeStats.js ('financeStats') are duplicate income/expense systems; pick the ledger (it has a schema, ledger.schema.json), migrate financeStats data in, and rebuild FinanceStatsPage dashboards as computed views over the ledger. Add donorId/grantId/campaignId foreign keys to ledger entries so 'Donations' and 'Grants & Funding' category rows trace to their source, enabling real revenue-by-source reporting, 990 prep, and restricted-fund accounting.

revamp_recommendations.title: Real-time auction bidding and lead-to-donor conversion in the inbox
revamp_recommendations.detail: Auction: add a bids table to the website worker (itemId, name, email, amountCents, createdAt), show current high bid on fundraiser.html cards, and auto-notify outbid/winning bidders; record winner payments as donations with FMV quid-pro-quo receipts. Inbox: in SB-v2's Inbox/contacts view, add a 'Convert to donor' / 'Create major-gift opportunity' action for contact rows with subject donate/in-kind/car donation, creating the donor record and a follow-up task with an owner — so corporate.html's 'Start a conversation' CTA feeds a pipeline instead of an archive.

strengths: donate.html is genuinely strong conversion copywriting: concrete impact anchors ($25/$75/$500 strip), the Charlie story with a real dollar figure, interactive impact slider with one-time/monthly toggle, trust indicators (EIN, 501(c)(3), '100% volunteer-run') repeated at every decision point, FAQ objection handling, and a tasteful exit-intent overlay that respects donate-intent sessionStorage — preserve all of this.

Donate-click tracking is already instrumented client-side (app.js SF.track, lines 101-113, auto-detecting zeffy/paypal/donorbox/givebutter hrefs with location + amount props) — it only needs an analytics backend enabled.

The SB-v2 → website admin proxy (functions/api/site/[[path]].js with server-side Firebase role verification and the site admin token never reaching the browser) is a clean, secure integration pattern — the exact channel to reuse for campaign thermometers, donation webhooks, and lead sync.

Multi-channel giving is well merchandised: online, check, ACH, in-kind, vehicle, monthly, corporate matching, sponsorship, and auction all have clear entry points across donate.html, corporate.html, ways-to-help.html.

The AI Letter-of-Intent generator (functions/api/generate-loi.js on Workers AI) and the curated grant-finder catalog are ahead of what most small rescues have — they just need persistence behind them.

Newsletter capture is a real end-to-end pipeline (pre-footer form on every page → POST /api/newsletter → Neon newsletter_subscribers with source attribution → admin-only export) — a working template for how donation data should flow.

Consistent Pinia store CRUD conventions and the ledger JSON schema (ledger.schema.json) mean new fundraising stores (donations, campaigns, pledges, grants) can follow an established, documented pattern.

expert: Nonprofit CMS / volunteer & donation workflow specialist (Morweb-style)
integration_assessment: The website and app do not share a single byte of volunteer data. The website's rich 4-step volunteer application (volunteer-signup.html: name, guardian/waiver flags for minors, interests, transport/vehicle, days/times/frequency, experience, heat tolerance, accessibility needs) is flattened into a plain-text string by formatVolunteerMessage() and POSTed to /api/contact, which inserts it into a Neon Postgres `contacts` table (_worker.js lines 14655-14674) — the same generic inbox as adoption, donation, and general messages. SanctuaryBase runs entirely on Firebase Firestore and never reads that table; its `applications` store (SB- v2/src/stores/applications.js) queries a Firestore `applications` collection the website never writes to. The only 'integration' is a hyperlink: the website thank-you screen and volunteer.html point at https://sanctuarybase.pages.dev/ where the volunteer re-enters name, email, phone, availability, skills, and experience from scratch in VolunteerSignup.vue — a second, competing application with different taxonomies (website: care/socialization/transport/maintenance/fostering/admin interests; app: Animal care/Cleaning/Feeding/Socializing/Medical/Admin skills). Worse, volunteer.html and volunteer-signup.html present two different recruitment funnels simultaneously (one says 'sign up on SanctuaryBase', the other hosts its own form), so applicants split across two disconnected systems and the coordinator must manually reconcile the contacts inbox against Firestore people records. Nothing flows back the other way either: no application status, waiver status, or volunteer hours ever surface on the website side.

top_findings.title: Volunteer applications are destroyed into unstructured text at the point of capture
top_findings.evidence: SaintFrancis-Website/volunteer-signup.html lines 792-846 (formatVolunteerMessage + submitForm POST to /api/contact with subject:'volunteer'); _worker.js lines 14655-14674 (contactsTable insert: name, email, subject, message only)
top_findings.detail: The website form collects ~18 structured fields (availability days/times, frequency, interests, minor/guardian data, accessibility needs) then serializes them into a newline-joined message string stored in a generic contacts table. All structure is lost; nothing can be queried, matched to shifts, or imported into SanctuaryBase without a human parsing text. The fetch even swallows errors (.catch(function(){})) and shows the thank-you screen regardless, so failed submissions are silently lost with no retry and no record.

top_findings.title: Two disconnected volunteer intake funnels compete with each other
top_findings.evidence: volunteer.html lines 87, 214-266 (CTAs send people to sanctuarybase.pages.dev to sign up); volunteer-signup.html hosts its own 4-step form posting to /api/contact; SB- v2/src/components/auth/VolunteerSignup.vue is a separate 3-step signup writing to Firestore people + waivers
top_findings.detail: A prospective volunteer can end up in Neon Postgres (website form), in Firestore (app signup), or both, with different field taxonomies. The website thank-you screen then asks them to 'Create your account' in SanctuaryBase and re-type everything they just submitted. There is no dedupe, no handoff token, no import — the classic double-entry break in the recruit-to-onboard pipeline.

top_findings.title: Two parallel, incompatible hour-tracking systems inside the app itself
top_findings.evidence: SB- v2/src/stores/shifts.js (clockIn/clockOut write to 'shifts' collection, hours as decimal, used by Shifts.vue lines 246-261); src/stores/clockIn.js (clockIn/clockOut write to 'checkins' collection, duration in minutes, used by auth.js clockIn lines 124-152)
top_findings.detail: Clocking in from the Shifts page creates a 'shifts' doc; clocking in via the auth-store path creates a 'checkins' doc. Neither reads the other, so total-hours reports (clockIn.getAllVolunteerHours vs Shifts.vue stats reduce) disagree depending on which button the volunteer used. Any hour-recognition or grant-reporting number built on this is unreliable.

top_findings.title: Volunteer shift stats leak/miscount: 'my shifts' actually returns everyone's shifts
top_findings.evidence: SB- v2/src/stores/shifts.js line 15: `const volunteerShifts = computed(() => shifts.value)`; consumed by Shifts.vue lines 223-232 for upcoming/past/totalHours; also the persistent 'volunterId' (sic) typo used as the field name throughout shifts.js and Shifts.vue
top_findings.detail: The volunteer view's upcoming shifts, past shifts, and total-hours stats are computed over the entire shifts collection, not filtered by the logged-in user (getVolunteerShifts exists but is unused here). Combined with the misspelled volunterId field baked into stored documents, this makes personal hour reporting wrong and future migration painful.

top_findings.title: Applications store is orphaned — nothing feeds it and no review workflow uses volunteer data
top_findings.evidence: SB- v2/src/stores/applications.js (full CRUD against Firestore 'applications' with status pending/approved) — but the website writes to Neon contacts, and VolunteerSignup.vue writes directly to 'people' with status:'active' (line 449) with no review gate
top_findings.detail: The app has the skeleton of an application pipeline (fetchApplicationsByStatus, addApplication), but volunteer signups bypass it entirely: anyone who completes the in-app form becomes an 'active' volunteer person record instantly, with no coordinator approval step, no onboarding checklist, no training gate. The pending-review concept exists only for adoption/foster applications seeded manually.

top_findings.title: Waivers are self-attested text signatures with no minor-permission path
top_findings.evidence: SB- v2/src/stores/waivers.js (signature stored as typed string, waiverVersion '1.0' hardcoded in VolunteerSignup.vue line 435); volunteer-signup.html minor flow (lines 429-449) promises 'We'll email the waiver to your parent or guardian' but no email or waiver record is ever created
top_findings.detail: The website tells under-18 applicants a guardian waiver will be emailed — there is no code anywhere that sends it or tracks it. In the app, the waiver is a typed-name match check (signature.toLowerCase() === name.toLowerCase()) with no versioning workflow, no re-sign on version change, and no expiry. For a liability document at an animal sanctuary (bite risk is real — the app has a biteReports store), this is a compliance gap.

top_findings.title: Role assignment and onboarding state are disconnected from the volunteer record
top_findings.evidence: SB- v2/src/stores/auth.js resolveUserRole (users/{uid} doc, default 'volunteer', roleInvites queue); userRoles.js setRoleForEmail; people.js addPerson — users, people, and waivers are three unlinked collections keyed differently (uid vs email vs name)
top_findings.detail: A volunteer exists as a users doc (auth/role), a people doc (profile), a waivers doc, plus shifts/checkins rows — joined only by fuzzy email/name matching. There is no canonical volunteer entity, so 'is this person onboarded, waiver-signed, trained, and active?' cannot be answered with one query.

top_findings.title: No scheduling in the sense the website promises — shifts are clock-in records, not bookable slots
top_findings.evidence: volunteer.html line 216 promises 'see what shifts are open, and claim one'; SB- v2 Shifts.vue volunteer view shows only 'Your upcoming shifts' created by an admin and an EmptyState saying 'Check with your coordinator for the schedule' — there is no open-shift board, no claim/signup action on shifts (only tasks in VolunteerHub.vue are claimable)
top_findings.detail: The public marketing promises self-service shift booking that the app does not implement. Admins can create shifts for a named volunteer, and volunteers can claim ad-hoc tasks, but there is no capacity-based schedule (e.g., 'Saturday AM feeding, 3 slots') that volunteers can browse and book — the core feature of every volunteer-management platform.

revamp_recommendations.title: One intake pipeline: website form writes structured applications directly into SanctuaryBase
revamp_recommendations.detail: Replace the /api/contact text-dump with a dedicated /api/volunteer-applications endpoint (Cloudflare function using Firebase Admin REST or a signed webhook) that writes the full structured payload from volunteer-signup.html into the Firestore 'applications' collection with type:'volunteer', status:'pending'. Retire the duplicate in-app application questions from VolunteerSignup.vue — when the applicant later creates their account, match on email and hydrate their people profile from the application (interests → skills, availDays/availTimes → availability, guardian/minor flags, accessibility needs). One form, zero re-entry, and the coordinator reviews applications inside the app instead of a raw contacts inbox.

revamp_recommendations.title: Canonical volunteer entity with lifecycle states
revamp_recommendations.detail: Introduce a single volunteers model (or harden 'people' with type:'volunteer') keyed to auth uid, carrying a status machine: applied → approved → waiver_signed → orientation_complete → active → inactive/alumni. Link users/{uid}, waivers, checkins, shifts, and tasks to it by uid, not email/name. Add an onboarding checklist sub-document (waiver, safety briefing, heat training, minor permission if applicable) so the coordinator dashboard can show exactly who is stuck at which step — this is the backbone every other module (scheduling, hours, recognition) hangs off.

revamp_recommendations.title: Real self-service shift scheduling with capacity slots
revamp_recommendations.detail: Build the shift model the website already advertises: shiftTemplates (e.g., 'Sat 6-9am feeding, capacity 3, requires: orientation_complete') generating bookable occurrences, a volunteer-facing open-shift board with one-tap claim/release, coordinator override, and reminder notifications. Gate claimable shifts on lifecycle state and certifications (e.g., med-run shifts require 'Medical' skill verified). Reuse the recurringTasks materialization pattern (recurringTasks.js) but move generation server-side (a Cloudflare cron worker) so it does not depend on an admin happening to open the app.

revamp_recommendations.title: Unify hour tracking into a single timesheet service
revamp_recommendations.detail: Collapse the shifts.js and clockIn.js clock-in/out paths into one 'timeEntries' collection (uid, shiftId?, source, clockInAt, clockOutAt, minutes), migrate existing 'shifts' and 'checkins' docs, fix the volunterId typo and the volunteerShifts computed that returns all shifts (shifts.js line 15). On top of it, add hour reports: per-volunteer YTD, org-wide totals for grant applications, exportable CSV, and an in-kind labor valuation line that can feed the ledger/financeStats stores the app already has.

revamp_recommendations.title: Waiver and training/certification module with versioning and minor support
revamp_recommendations.detail: Version waiver documents (store text + version in Firestore), require re-sign when the version bumps, capture drawn or typed signature with timestamp and IP, and add an expiry/renewal cycle. Implement the guardian flow the website promises: a minor application triggers an emailed signing link (Cloudflare Email Service) to the guardian, and the volunteer cannot pass 'waiver_signed' until it returns. Extend the same engine to training certifications (heat safety, large-animal handling, med administration) with expiry dates that scheduling can enforce.

revamp_recommendations.title: Recognition and retention layer
revamp_recommendations.detail: The website sells 'watch your hours and your impact add up' — deliver it: milestone badges (10/50/100 hours), an annual hours letter (volunteers need these for school/court/employer programs), streak tracking off timeEntries, and coordinator nudges for volunteers who have not clocked in for 30 days. Surface an opt-in public counter on the website ('22 volunteers, 4,100 hours this year') fed by a small public stats endpoint, replacing the hardcoded volunteers: 22 in _worker.js /api/stats.

revamp_recommendations.title: Coordinator inbox: merge website contacts into the app
revamp_recommendations.detail: The app's Inbox.vue and the website admin.html contacts inbox are separate tools with separate auth (Firestore rules vs a shared ADMIN_TOKEN bearer string). Sync or proxy the Neon contacts (adoption/foster/donation/general inquiries) into the app's inbox so staff work one queue, and replace the static ADMIN_TOKEN with Firebase-auth-verified access. This also lets a 'convert to volunteer application / adopter / donor' action create the right structured record from any inquiry.

revamp_recommendations.title: Close the loop on the two-funnel confusion now (cheap interim fix)
revamp_recommendations.detail: Until the pipeline is rebuilt: make volunteer.html and volunteer-signup.html agree on one path (form first, account second); pass the applicant's email as a query param to sanctuarybase.pages.dev so the app signup pre-fills and tags the account; make the website form surface submission failures instead of the silent .catch(function(){}); and remove the unused quick-interest sessionStorage widget (volunteer-signup.html lines 218-233) whose answers are never read or submitted anywhere.

strengths: The website volunteer form itself is excellent nonprofit UX: warm copy, 4-step progressive disclosure, minor/guardian branch, accessibility-needs question, transport/vehicle follow-up, and only name+email+age required (volunteer-signup.html) — the data model it captures is exactly what a volunteer CRM needs; only the storage is wrong.

The app's in-signup waiver step (VolunteerSignup.vue steps 3-4) correctly blocks account completion until a signature matching the legal name is provided, and writes an auditable waivers record with signedAt and waiverVersion.

The roleInvites pattern (userRoles.js + auth.js resolveUserRole) is a genuinely clean solution for pre-assigning roles to people who have not logged in yet, enforced by Firestore rules rather than client trust.

recurringTasks.js shows thoughtful engineering for a no-server-cron environment: period keys, lastGeneratedKey stamping, and a second duplicate guard on (recurringId, periodKey) — a solid pattern to reuse for shift generation.
VolunteerHub.vue's claim/complete/release task flow with real-time onSnapshot updates (tasks.js) is a working, volunteer-friendly micro-volunteering loop worth keeping as-is.

The website consistently markets SanctuaryBase as the volunteer home base (volunteer.html 'Meet SanctuaryBase' section, PWA install hints on the thank-you screen) — the brand narrative for a website-plus-app ecosystem is already in place; the plumbing just has to catch up.

expert: Robintek/Equalize-style senior developer & remediation auditor — architecture, performance, and security for animal-services platforms
integration_assessment: The two systems connect through one well-designed seam and several broken or missing ones. The good seam: SanctuaryBase's Pages Function proxy (`SB- v2/functions/api/site/[[path]].js` + `functions/_shared/verifyRole.js`) verifies the caller's Firebase ID token server-side against their Firestore role, then forwards to the website's Hono worker API with a `SITE_ADMIN_TOKEN` secret that never reaches the browser — this is the right pattern and it powers the app's Inbox (Neon contacts), website animal listings (WebsiteListingTab), and auction items. The broken seams: (1) the website's adoption and foster applications POST structured fields (phone, address, experience, setup, care, goals) to `/api/contact`, but the worker's insert at `_worker.js:14655` persists only name/email/subject/message — most of the application data is silently destroyed; (2) the app maintains a separate Firestore `applications` collection and ApplicationsPage that never receives website submissions, so there are two disconnected application pipelines; (3) animals exist twice — Firestore `animals` for operations and Neon `animals` for public listings — synced only by manual per-animal pushes; (4) donations go through Zeffy links with no webhook, so the app's donors/ledger/financeStats modules are hand-fed and never see actual online donations. The website is essentially a brochure with a mail slot; for the "front door" vision, applications, donations, and animal status need structured, automatic flow into SanctuaryBase.

top_findings.title: Adoption/foster application data silently discarded server-side
top_findings.evidence: SaintFrancis-Website/adopt-apply.html submit handler (~line 464-495) sends phone, address, interest, experience, setup, other-animals, care, goals; foster-apply.html (~line 452) sends animals, living, experience, time. _worker.js POST /contact (line 14655) inserts ONLY name, email, subject, message into contactsTable.
top_findings.detail: Every adoption and foster application loses the applicant's phone number, address, and all screening answers the moment it is submitted — staff see only the free-text message in the SanctuaryBase Inbox. The volunteer form (volunteer-signup.html:815-845) avoids this by flattening everything into the message string, but it also swallows all errors (.catch(function(){}) then shows success), so failed submissions look successful to the applicant. This is active data loss on the org's most important intake flows.

top_findings.title: Unauthenticated Cloudflare Functions expose R2 documents, AI, and an open email relay
top_findings.evidence: SB- v2/functions/api/: delete-document.js, upload-document.js, get-documents.js, vet-chat.js, generate-loi.js, send-reminder.js have no auth check; grep shows only generate-animal-bio.js and api/site/* use verifyCallerRole/Authorization.
top_findings.detail: Anyone on the internet can DELETE any object in the GRANTS_BUCKET R2 bucket (delete-document.js takes an arbitrary ?key=), list and upload grant documents, burn Workers AI quota via vet-chat/generate-loi, and use send-reminder as an open email relay (arbitrary to/subject/body → spam sent under the org's domain). The verifyCallerRole helper already exists and is proven in the site proxy — it simply is not applied to these six endpoints.

top_findings.title: Firestore rules leak donor, finance, and private-message data to every volunteer
top_findings.evidence: SB- v2/firestore.rules: ledgers, donors, donations, financeStats, grants, biteReports, eodReports all 'allow read: if isAuth()'; messages 'allow read: if isAuth()' (line 444) means any user reads all DMs; tasks/swaps/events/availability/supplies 'allow update/write: if isAuth()'.
top_findings.detail: Any self-registered volunteer account (signup creates role 'volunteer' automatically per stores/auth.js) can read the full donor CRM, the complete financial ledger, everyone's direct messages, and bite/incident reports, and can update any task, shift swap, event, or supply record. The role-invite mechanism and default-deny rule are genuinely well designed, but read-scoping was never done per role. For a platform holding donor PII and finances, reads must be tiered like writes already are.

top_findings.title: No router: tab-state navigation cannot scale to a full platform
top_findings.evidence: SB- v2/src/App.vue lines 37-139: 50 statically imported page components in a routeMap keyed by ui.currentTab; src/stores/ui.js: currentTab/selectedAnimal are plain refs with no URL or history sync.
top_findings.detail: There is no vue-router: no deep links (staff cannot bookmark or share 'animal Leo, Health tab'), the browser back button exits the app, a refresh on AnimalDetail loses ui.selectedAnimal and dumps the user to the dashboard, and there are no route-level guards — admin gating is repeated ad hoc inside components. Every new module makes App.vue's import list and routeMap longer. This is the single biggest structural blocker to the powerhouse vision.

top_findings.title: Single 1.18 MB JS bundle, no code splitting, empty vite config
top_findings.evidence: SB- v2/dist/assets/index-BKSqHvuh.js = 1,182,368 bytes plus index-DIjZRvtX.css = 331,939 bytes; vite.config.js has no manualChunks, no rollup output config; all 50 pages eagerly imported in App.vue (only recurringTasks store is dynamically imported).
top_findings.detail: A volunteer on a phone in a barn downloads the entire admin suite — grants, finance stats, bite reports, auction admin — before seeing the dashboard. Firebase SDK alone is several hundred KB and is bundled with everything else. With a router and per-route dynamic imports plus a vendor chunk for firebase/vue/pinia, first-load JS could realistically drop 60-70%. Also no PWA/service worker despite field use on mobile.

top_findings.title: Fetch-everything Firestore access: no pagination, no cache, no offline support
top_findings.evidence: 46 getDocs() calls across 29 stores (e.g. src/stores/animals.js fetchAnimals reads the whole 'animals' collection); only activityLog.js and roundsHistory.js use limit(); services/firebase.js uses plain getFirestore() with no persistentLocalCache; only 3 stores (tasks, announcements, supplyInventory) use onSnapshot.
top_findings.detail: Nearly every page load re-reads entire collections. Fine at 50 animals; at multi-year scale (feeding logs, ledger entries, rounds history grow unboundedly) this means slow pages and mounting Firestore read costs. There is no IndexedDB persistence, so the app is unusable offline — a real problem for a sanctuary with patchy connectivity. The three onSnapshot stores do guard against double-subscription (tasks.js firstSnapshotPromise) which is good, but module-level listeners are never stopped on logout.

top_findings.title: Website worker is a 492 KB compiled bundle committed as source
top_findings.evidence: SaintFrancis-Website/_worker.js (492,333 bytes) contains bundled Hono + Drizzle with hand-maintained routes starting ~line 14625; no src/ directory or build script for it in the repo.
top_findings.detail: Application routes are edited directly inside a minified-ish vendor bundle. Any dependency upgrade or non-trivial change risks corrupting the file, diffs are unreviewable, and there are no tests possible. The vestigial POST /admin/login (line 14856) still returns the raw ADMIN_TOKEN when the 'password' equals the token — pointless now that admin.html is a redirect stub, and a needless token-echo endpoint.

top_findings.title: Public forms have no bot protection or rate limiting
top_findings.evidence: _worker.js POST /contact (14655), /newsletter (14676), /suggestions (14760) accept unauthenticated JSON with no Turnstile, no rate limiting, no honeypot; grep for turnstile/captcha/rate in _worker.js finds nothing.
top_findings.detail: The contact table (which is also the app's Inbox) and newsletter list can be flooded by trivial scripts, polluting the exact data staff triage in SanctuaryBase. Cloudflare Turnstile is free and native to this stack.

revamp_recommendations.title: Adopt vue-router with lazy-loaded route modules
revamp_recommendations.detail: Replace ui.currentTab with vue-router: one route per current tab (paths like /animals/:id/health), route-level code splitting via () => import(...), a navigation guard reading auth.user.role for admin/* routes, and query/param state instead of ui.selectedAnimal. This unlocks deep links, back/refresh, shareable URLs, per-module bundles, and makes every future module (HR, events, comms) a self-contained folder of routes + stores + components instead of another App.vue import. Migration is mechanical because routeMap in App.vue already is a router table.

revamp_recommendations.title: Build a real intake pipeline: structured applications end-to-end
revamp_recommendations.detail: Fix the /contact data loss immediately (persist the full JSON payload, e.g. a jsonb 'details' column in Neon or dedicated applications table), then go further: add POST /api/applications on the website worker with typed adoption/foster/volunteer schemas, have SanctuaryBase's ApplicationsPage read them through the existing site proxy (or sync into the Firestore 'applications' collection), and wire status transitions (submitted → screening → approved → adopted) back to the animal record. This turns the website into the actual front door instead of a mail slot, and merges the two disconnected application pipelines.

revamp_recommendations.title: Lock down the functions layer and re-tier Firestore reads
revamp_recommendations.detail: Apply verifyCallerRole (already written in functions/_shared/verifyRole.js) to all six unauthenticated functions — delete/upload/get-document should require admin, vet-chat/generate-loi staff, send-reminder staff with an allowlisted sender. In firestore.rules, change donors/ledgers/donations/financeStats/grants/biteReports reads to isPrivileged(), scope messages reads to sender/recipient, and tighten the isAuth() writes on tasks/swaps/events/supplies to owner-or-privileged. Keep the excellent roleInvites + default-deny design as-is.

revamp_recommendations.title: Code-split and add offline support for field use
revamp_recommendations.detail: With the router in place: dynamic-import every page, add manualChunks in vite.config.js (firebase, vue/pinia vendors), and switch services/firebase.js to initializeFirestore with persistentLocalCache so the app works in the barn with no signal (feeding logs, rounds, meds queue offline and sync). Add a PWA manifest + service worker so staff install it on phones. Target: <300 KB initial JS.

revamp_recommendations.title: Close the donation loop with a payment webhook and donor sync
revamp_recommendations.detail: Zeffy links mean online donations never reach the app's donors/ledger/financeStats stores. Add a webhook receiver (Zeffy Zapier export, or move to Stripe/Givebutter with native webhooks) as a Cloudflare Function that writes donation records and upserts donors — making the fundraising module real instead of manually maintained. Same pattern later feeds newsletter signups into a communications module (the Neon newsletter table already exists and the app already has NewsletterPage).

revamp_recommendations.title: Un-bundle the website worker into buildable source
revamp_recommendations.detail: Extract the ~500 lines of actual routes (lines 14625+) into a src/worker/ project with Hono + Drizzle as npm dependencies and a wrangler/esbuild build step producing _worker.js. Delete the vestigial /admin/login token-echo route. This makes the API testable and safely evolvable — a prerequisite for the richer /api/applications and webhook endpoints above.

revamp_recommendations.title: Introduce pagination and query discipline in stores
revamp_recommendations.detail: Add orderBy + limit + startAfter cursors to the unbounded fetchAll stores (feedingLogs, ledger, rounds, medicalRecords, eodReports grow forever), standardize a shared createCollectionStore factory (the 40 stores are near-identical copies of the same 100-line pattern — a factory cuts thousands of duplicated lines and gives one place to add caching/pagination/error handling), and prefer where-scoped queries (per-animal, per-date-range) over whole-collection reads.

revamp_recommendations.title: Add Turnstile and rate limiting to public website forms
revamp_recommendations.detail: Protect /api/contact, /api/newsletter, /api/suggestions with Cloudflare Turnstile server-side verification plus a simple per-IP rate limit in the worker; also fix volunteer-signup.html's swallowed fetch errors so applicants see real failure states. Keeps the SanctuaryBase Inbox clean as traffic grows.

strengths: The site-proxy security pattern (functions/api/site/[[path]].js + _shared/verifyRole.js): Firebase ID token verified server-side against the real Firestore role, site admin token kept as a Pages secret, staff/admin path scoping, and careful byte-for-byte multipart passthrough — this is the correct template for all future website↔app integration.

Firestore rules fundamentals: default-deny catch-all, role stored in users/{uid} as single source of truth with no self-escalation, and the roleInvites flow verified inside the rules via get() rather than trusted from the client — thoughtful, documented design.

The public website's performance and hardening posture is genuinely strong: preloaded hero image and self-hosted woff2 fonts, webp images, deferred analytics, tight CSP + HSTS + full security-header set in _headers, immutable caching for static assets, noscript fallbacks, and schema.org structured data.

App.vue's routeMap object (vs. 45 v-else-ifs) and the guarded onSnapshot subscription pattern in tasks.js (firstSnapshotPromise prevents duplicate listeners) show the codebase is already halfway to router/listener discipline.
Retiring the website's own admin.html to a redirect stub and consolidating all admin into SanctuaryBase eliminated a whole duplicate attack surface and duplicate UI to maintain.

Documentation depth (ARCHITECTURE.md, CODEBASE_MAP.md, DESIGN_SYSTEM.md, seed-data guides) and a consistent UI component library (src/components/ui) give a solid base for onboarding contributors to a larger platform.
